passage_text:
Dr. Elara Vance, born in 1905 in Maine, was a pioneering marine biologist who revolutionized the study of deep-sea ecosystems. From a young age, Vance spent her days exploring tide pools, which ignited her lifelong passion for the ocean. Despite her parents' wishes for her to pursue a more traditional career, she earned a degree in marine biology from a prestigious university in 1927.

In 1932, Vance made her most significant contribution to science by developing the 'Aquasphere,' a heavily armored diving bell that allowed researchers to observe marine life at unprecedented depths. Using this groundbreaking invention, she discovered over fifty new species of deep-sea creatures. Her detailed observations were published in her 1935 book, Mysteries of the Deep, which became a massive success and brought her international fame.

However, Vance's journey was not without obstacles. During the 1940s, she struggled to secure financial backing for her expeditions due to the economic downturn and widespread skepticism from her male colleagues. Many scientists dismissed her findings, claiming that life could not survive in the extreme pressure of the deep ocean.

Undeterred, Vance established the Vance Marine Institute in 1950, dedicating the rest of her life to ocean conservation and funding young researchers. She passed away in 1980, leaving behind a legacy that continues to inspire modern marine biologists.

questions.quiz: What did Elara Vance's parents want her to do?
questions.answer3: Travel the world
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'Despite her parents' wishes for her to pursue a more traditional career'라는 표현이 나옵니다. traditional career(전통적인 직업)를 보기에서는 conventional career(관습적인 직업)로 paraphrasing했습니다. 지텔프에서 자주 쓰이는 paraphrasing 패턴이니 꼭 익혀두세요.

questions.quiz: What was the main purpose of the Aquasphere?
questions.answer3: To observe marine life at great depths
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'allowed researchers to observe marine life at unprecedented depths'라고 명시되어 있습니다. unprecedented(전례 없는)는 지텔프 독해에서 자주 등장하는 형용사입니다. 보기의 'at great depths'는 지문의 'at unprecedented depths'를 paraphrasing한 표현입니다.

questions.quiz: What is true about Vance's book published in 1935?
questions.answer1: It was ignored by the public
questions.answer3: It was a work of fiction
questions.commentary: 2단락에서 책에는 50종 이상의 심해 생물 관찰 내용이 담겼고, 'massive success(큰 성공)'를 거뒀다고 합니다. 보기 1번 'ignored(무시됨)'는 지문과 정반대이므로 오답입니다. 지텔프에서는 지문 내용과 정반대인 보기로 함정을 만드는 경우가 많으니 주의하세요.

questions.quiz: Why did Vance have difficulty getting funding in the 1940s?
questions.answer1: The Aquasphere invention was too dangerous
questions.answer2: She had lost all her savings
questions.answer3: Economic downturn and skepticism from colleagues
questions.answer4: She had already retired from research
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'due to the economic downturn and widespread skepticism from her male colleagues'라고 두 가지 이유가 명확하게 나옵니다. 이처럼 이유를 묻는 문제는 'due to / because of / owing to' 뒤에 답이 오는 경우가 많습니다.

questions.quiz: What did Vance do in 1950?
questions.answer3: Retired from scientific work
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'Vance established the Vance Marine Institute in 1950'이라고 나옵니다. established(설립했다)를 보기에서는 founded(설립했다)로 paraphrasing했습니다. established = founded는 지텔프 독해 paraphrasing의 대표적인 예입니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'groundbreaking' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: complicated
questions.commentary: groundbreaking은 '획기적인, 혁신적인'이라는 뜻으로, innovative(혁신적인)와 동의어입니다. 반의어는 conventional / traditional(전통적인)입니다. 2단락에서 Aquasphere를 설명하는 맥락이므로, 세상에 없던 새로운 발명품을 수식하는 단어임을 파악하면 쉽게 고를 수 있습니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'undeterred' in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: confused
questions.commentary: undeterred는 '굴하지 않고, 단념하지 않고'라는 뜻입니다. deter(단념시키다, 막다)에 부정 접두사 un-이 붙은 형태로, unshaken(흔들리지 않는)과 의미가 가장 가깝습니다. 1번 discouraged(낙담한)는 반의어에 해당하므로 오답입니다.

passage_text:
Julian Sterling, born in 1888 in a small village in France, was a revolutionary painter who paved the way for modern Impressionism. Growing up in a modest household, Sterling was initially discouraged by his father from pursuing art, with the family insisting he study medicine instead. Nevertheless, Sterling spent his nights sketching the local landscape, driven by an undeniable creative urge that would eventually define his career.

In 1912, Sterling achieved his major breakthrough when he began experimenting with light and shadow in his outdoor paintings. He developed a technique that focused on capturing the fleeting quality of light, which he termed 'Luminous Impression.' His first major exhibition in 1915 attracted thousands of visitors and brought him immediate fame, though traditional critics remained skeptical of his unconventional methods.

However, Sterling faced immense personal and professional hardship during the First World War. His studio was destroyed by an air raid in 1917, and he lost a vast collection of his early sketches. During this difficult time, his mental health suffered, and many doubted he would ever produce a masterpiece again.

Sterling eventually returned to his art with renewed vigor, producing his most iconic works in the 1920s. He later dedicated his remaining years to teaching young artists at the Paris Academy of Fine Arts before passing away in 1955. His legacy remains a cornerstone of 20th-century art.

questions.quiz: What career path did Julian Sterling's father originally want him to follow?
questions.answer3: Farming
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'the family insisting he study medicine instead'라고 직접적으로 언급되어 있습니다. insist(고집하다, 주장하다)는 지텔프에서 that절 동사원형과 함께 자주 출제됩니다. 'insist that + 주어 + 동사원형' 구조를 꼭 기억해 두세요.

questions.quiz: What is the 'Luminous Impression' technique known for?
questions.answer3: Capturing the fleeting quality of light
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'a technique that focused on capturing the fleeting quality of light'라고 정의되어 있습니다. fleeting(순간적인, 덧없는)은 지텔프 어휘 문제에 자주 나오는 단어입니다. 보기 1·2·4번은 지문에 전혀 언급되지 않은 내용으로 오답입니다.

questions.quiz: How did traditional critics react to Sterling's 1915 exhibition?
questions.answer2: They remained skeptical of his approach.
questions.answer3: They ignored the exhibition entirely.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'traditional critics remained skeptical of his unconventional methods'라고 나옵니다. skeptical(회의적인)은 비판적인 태도를 나타내는 핵심 표현입니다. 1번 praised(칭찬했다)는 지문 내용과 정반대이므로 오답입니다.

questions.quiz: What major setback did Sterling experience in 1917?
questions.answer1: His studio was destroyed by an air raid.
questions.answer2: He was drafted into the military.
questions.answer3: He permanently lost his eyesight.
questions.answer4: His financial support was cut off.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'His studio was destroyed by an air raid in 1917'라고 명확히 나옵니다. setback(좌절, 차질)은 지문에서 hardship / obstacle과 같은 역경을 표현하는 단어들과 같이 쓰이는 어휘입니다. 나머지 보기들은 지문에 언급되지 않은 내용입니다.

questions.quiz: What did Sterling do in his later years?
questions.answer1: He retired to a farm in the countryside.
questions.answer3: He wrote a detailed autobiography.
questions.answer4: He taught at the Paris Academy of Fine Arts.
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'dedicated his remaining years to teaching young artists at the Paris Academy of Fine Arts'라고 나옵니다. dedicated A to B(A를 B에 헌신했다) 구조를 기억해 두세요. 지텔프 독해에서 마지막 단락에 인물의 말년 행적이 나오는 패턴이 자주 출제됩니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'modest' in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: humble
questions.commentary: modest는 '검소한, 소박한, 변변치 않은'이라는 뜻으로, humble(소박한, 수수한)이 동의어입니다. 1번 wealthy(부유한)는 반의어에 해당합니다. 'a modest household'는 '소박한 가정'이라는 의미로, Sterling이 부유하지 않은 환경에서 자랐음을 나타냅니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'vigor' in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: caution
questions.commentary: vigor는 '활력, 열정, 힘'이라는 뜻으로, energy(에너지, 활기)가 동의어입니다. 'returned to his art with renewed vigor'는 '새로운 활력으로 예술로 돌아왔다'는 의미입니다. vigorous(활기찬)라는 형용사 형태도 함께 기억해 두세요.

passage_text:
Marcus Montague, born in 1895 in Italy, emerged as one of the most influential political reformers of his time. Born into a family of laborers, Montague witnessed the systemic inequality in his community, which inspired him to enter the world of law. He aimed to challenge the corrupt political machinery that burdened the working class with unfair taxes.

Montague's greatest political victory came in 1928 with the passage of the Labor Protection Act. This legislation set unprecedented standards for workplace safety and minimum wages, directly benefiting millions of workers across the nation. His ability to negotiate with rival parties was key to passing such a controversial bill, earning him a reputation as a master diplomat.

However, Montague's rise to power sparked fierce opposition from the political elite. In 1934, he faced a massive smear campaign orchestrated by his opponents, who accused him of corruption to undermine his popularity. The scandal forced him to resign from his position, leaving his reform movement in a state of chaos.

Despite the setback, Montague continued to write and speak out against inequality until his death in 1970. His life is still studied by historians as a symbol of integrity and commitment to social justice.

questions.quiz: What motivated Montague to enter the field of law?
questions.answer1: Witnessing systemic inequality in his community
questions.answer2: His family's wealth and political connections
questions.answer3: A personal desire to become a politician
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'witnessed the systemic inequality in his community, which inspired him to enter the world of law'라고 나옵니다. which는 관계대명사로 앞 내용 전체를 받아 결과를 설명합니다. inspired(영감을 주다, 자극하다)는 motivated(동기를 부여하다)로 paraphrasing되었습니다.

questions.quiz: What was the outcome of the Labor Protection Act?
questions.answer1: Taxes were increased for working-class citizens
questions.answer2: Workplace safety and minimum wages were improved
questions.answer3: Rival political parties lost most of their power
questions.answer4: The government reduced its role in labor disputes
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'set unprecedented standards for workplace safety and minimum wages, directly benefiting millions of workers'라고 나옵니다. outcome(결과)을 묻는 문제에서는 지문의 result / effect / benefit 관련 표현을 찾으면 됩니다. 1번은 지문 내용과 반대입니다.

questions.quiz: How did Montague manage to pass the controversial bill?
questions.answer1: By ignoring criticism from his opponents
questions.answer2: By using his family's financial resources
questions.answer3: By successfully negotiating with rival parties
questions.answer4: By threatening members of the political elite
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'His ability to negotiate with rival parties was key to passing such a controversial bill'이라고 나옵니다. manage to(~을 해내다)는 succeed in과 같은 의미입니다. 이 문제에서 'key(핵심, 열쇠)'가 정답 근거가 되는 단어입니다.

questions.quiz: Why was Montague forced to resign in 1934?
questions.answer1: He was targeted by a smear campaign accusing him of corruption.
questions.answer2: He resigned due to serious health problems.
questions.answer3: He was offered a more prominent government position.
questions.answer4: He chose to retire from public life voluntarily.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'he faced a massive smear campaign orchestrated by his opponents, who accused him of corruption'이라고 나옵니다. smear campaign(비방 캠페인)과 accused of corruption(부패 혐의 고발)이 핵심 표현입니다. forced to resign(강제로 사임당하다)이라는 표현도 시험에 자주 등장합니다.

questions.quiz: How is Marcus Montague regarded today?
questions.answer1: As a politician who failed to achieve meaningful reform
questions.answer2: As a historian who documented social injustice
questions.answer3: As a corrupt official who betrayed the working class
questions.answer4: As a symbol of integrity and commitment to social justice
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'His life is still studied by historians as a symbol of integrity and commitment to social justice'라고 나옵니다. 전기문 마지막 단락에는 인물의 현재 평가가 담기는 경우가 많습니다. integrity(청렴함, 성실함)는 지텔프 독해에서 자주 나오는 어휘입니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'burdened' in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: rewarded
questions.commentary: burden은 명사로 '짐, 부담', 동사로 '짐을 지우다, 압박하다'라는 뜻입니다. 'weighed down(압박을 가하다, 짓누르다)'이 가장 가까운 의미입니다. 지문에서 'burdened the working class with unfair taxes'는 '불공정한 세금으로 노동자들을 압박했다'는 의미입니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'orchestrated' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: accidentally caused
questions.commentary: orchestrate는 원래 '오케스트라를 지휘하다'라는 뜻이지만, 비유적으로 '치밀하게 계획하고 조율하다'라는 의미로도 쓰입니다. 'a smear campaign orchestrated by his opponents'는 '적들에 의해 치밀하게 기획된 비방 캠페인'이라는 의미입니다.

passage_text:
Sylvia Hawthorne, an ecologist born in 1900 in Canada, spent her life defending the vast forests of the North. As a child, Hawthorne lived in a cabin where she developed a deep bond with the wilderness. While her family wanted her to focus on nursing, she chose to study forest ecology, believing that the future of the planet depended on the health of its natural resources.

Her defining moment arrived in 1938, when she spearheaded the campaign to save the Great Northern Forest from industrial logging. She organized thousands of citizens to lobby the government, eventually succeeding in designating the area as a protected national reserve. Her success made her a hero to nature lovers everywhere.

However, Hawthorne's life was met with significant backlash from powerful industrial corporations. In 1945, these companies launched a massive media campaign to discredit her, claiming her conservation efforts would ruin the local economy. For several years, she was barred from speaking at major universities.

Despite these trials, Hawthorne continued her work quietly. She established a forest research center in 1952, which focuses on sustainable land management. She remained a dedicated activist until she passed away in 1975, and her efforts are still a benchmark for modern conservationists.

questions.quiz: What did Hawthorne's family originally want her to pursue?
questions.answer3: A move to an urban area
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'While her family wanted her to focus on nursing'이라고 명시되어 있습니다. while(~인 반면에)은 대조를 나타내는 접속사로, 가족의 바람과 Hawthorne 본인의 선택이 대비되는 구조입니다. 지텔프 독해에서 while / although / however 뒤에 핵심 내용이 오는 경우가 많습니다.

questions.quiz: What was the result of Hawthorne's 1938 campaign?
questions.answer1: She was dismissed from her university position.
questions.answer2: The forest was cleared to allow industrial logging.
questions.answer3: The area was designated as a protected national reserve.
questions.answer4: She was appointed to a government advisory role.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'eventually succeeding in designating the area as a protected national reserve'라고 결과가 나옵니다. eventually(결국, 마침내)는 결과를 알려주는 신호 부사입니다. 2번은 지문 내용과 정반대(오답 함정)입니다.

questions.quiz: How did industrial corporations respond to Hawthorne's conservation work?
questions.answer1: They contributed funding to her research.
questions.answer2: They launched a media campaign to discredit her.
questions.answer3: They sought her advice on environmental policy.
questions.answer4: They offered her a high-paying position.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'these companies launched a massive media campaign to discredit her'라고 나옵니다. discredit(신뢰를 떨어뜨리다, 평판을 손상시키다)는 지텔프 독해에서 자주 나오는 부정적 표현입니다. 1·3·4번은 지문에 전혀 나오지 않는 내용입니다.

questions.quiz: What restriction did Hawthorne face in the years following 1945?
questions.answer1: She was barred from speaking at major universities.
questions.answer2: She was prohibited from publishing research.
questions.answer3: She was banned from entering national parks.
questions.answer4: She was denied funding for her research center.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'she was barred from speaking at major universities'라고 명시되어 있습니다. be barred from(~을 금지당하다)은 be prohibited from / be banned from과 같은 의미입니다. 나머지 보기들은 지문에 언급되지 않은 내용입니다.

questions.quiz: What was the focus of the research center Hawthorne established in 1952?
questions.answer3: Producing media content on environmental issues
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'a forest research center in 1952, which focuses on sustainable land management'라고 나옵니다. sustainable(지속 가능한)은 환경 관련 지문에서 매우 자주 등장하는 어휘입니다. 1번 logging(벌목)은 그녀가 반대했던 것이므로 오답 함정입니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'spearheaded' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: led
questions.commentary: spearhead는 원래 '창끝'이라는 뜻에서 파생되어 '선봉에 서다, 주도하다'라는 의미로 쓰입니다. led(이끌었다)가 동의어입니다. 유사 표현으로 headed, pioneered, initiated 등도 알아두면 유용합니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'backlash' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: public celebration
questions.commentary: backlash는 '강한 반발, 역풍'이라는 뜻으로, 어떤 행동이나 정책에 대한 부정적인 반응을 의미합니다. 'strong negative reaction(강한 부정적 반응)'이 가장 가까운 표현입니다. 1번 support(지지)는 반의어에 해당하므로 오답입니다.

passage_text:
Victor Caldwell, a visionary astronomer born in 1875 in Germany, changed how humanity perceives the night sky. From a young age, he spent nights observing constellations, yet his father, a wealthy merchant, insisted that he prepare for a career in law. Victor managed to study astronomy in secret, often staying awake until dawn to map the stars.

Caldwell's breakthrough came in 1905, when he developed a new method for calculating comet trajectories. By using advanced mathematics, he could predict the exact return times of rare comets. His findings were initially met with indifference by the scientific community, but they were soon verified by world-renowned experts, catapulting him to international fame.

Tragedy struck in 1912, when a lab fire destroyed many of his primary research journals and charts. This event set his work back significantly, as it took years for him to reconstruct the lost data from memory. During this time, he struggled with severe depression and nearly abandoned his research altogether.

He emerged stronger in 1920, opening a public observatory to encourage common people to engage with science. He continued his contributions to astronomy until his peaceful passing in 1947. Today, his formulas remain essential for space missions.

questions.quiz: What career did Victor Caldwell's father want him to pursue?
questions.answer3: Merchant trade
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'his father, a wealthy merchant, insisted that he prepare for a career in law'라고 나옵니다. insist that + 주어 + 동사원형(should 생략) 구조입니다. 3번 merchant(상인)는 아버지의 직업이지 아버지가 원한 직업이 아니므로 오답 함정입니다.

questions.quiz: What was Caldwell's major scientific achievement in 1905?
questions.answer1: He discovered a new planet in the solar system.
questions.answer2: He developed a method for calculating comet trajectories.
questions.answer3: He invented a telescope that could see through clouds.
questions.answer4: He built the world's largest public observatory.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'he developed a new method for calculating comet trajectories'라고 명시되어 있습니다. trajectory(궤도, 궤적)는 과학 관련 지문에서 자주 나오는 어휘입니다. 4번의 public observatory(공공 천문대)는 1920년의 일이므로 시기가 다른 오답 함정입니다.

questions.quiz: How did the scientific community initially respond to Caldwell's findings?
questions.answer2: They accused him of fabricating his results.
questions.answer3: They attempted to replicate his research.
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'His findings were initially met with indifference by the scientific community'라고 나옵니다. initially(처음에는)는 나중에 상황이 바뀔 것을 암시하는 부사입니다. 실제로 이후 'soon verified'로 상황이 반전됩니다. 이런 반전 구조를 파악하는 것이 지텔프 독해의 핵심입니다.

questions.quiz: What was the consequence of the 1912 lab fire for Caldwell?
questions.answer1: He lost critical research journals and charts.
questions.answer2: He permanently retired from scientific work.
questions.answer3: He suffered physical injuries in the fire.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'a lab fire destroyed many of his primary research journals and charts'라고 나옵니다. primary(주요한, 핵심적인)를 보기에서는 critical(중요한, 핵심적인)로 paraphrasing했습니다. 2번은 잘못된 내용으로, 그는 이후에도 연구를 계속했습니다.

questions.quiz: What did Caldwell open in 1920?
questions.answer2: A private laboratory for advanced astronomy
questions.answer3: A public observatory for the general public
questions.answer4: A scholarship foundation for young scientists
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'opening a public observatory to encourage common people to engage with science'라고 나옵니다. 목적을 나타내는 to부정사(to encourage)를 통해 공공 천문대를 연 이유도 함께 파악할 수 있습니다. common people = general public으로 paraphrasing되었습니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'indifference' in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: strong opposition
questions.commentary: indifference는 '무관심, 냉담함'이라는 뜻으로, 'lack of interest(관심 없음)'가 가장 가까운 의미입니다. indifferent(무관심한) 형용사 형태도 함께 기억해 두세요. 1번 excitement(흥분), 4번 admiration(감탄)은 반의어에 가깝습니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'reconstruct' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: destroy
questions.commentary: reconstruct는 're-(다시) + construct(만들다, 건설하다)'의 합성어로 '재구성하다, 다시 만들다'라는 뜻입니다. rebuild(재건하다)가 동의어입니다. 1번 abandon(포기하다), 3번 destroy(파괴하다)는 반의어에 해당합니다.

passage_text:
Clara Bennett, an English writer born in 1890, was famous for novels that challenged the social norms of the early 20th century. Born into a wealthy family, Bennett was expected to lead a quiet life of leisure. Instead, she began writing anonymous stories, using a masculine pen name to ensure her work was judged on its own merit.

Her debut novel, released in 1920, shocked the public with its raw portrayal of working-class struggles. Despite the controversy, the book was a best-seller, exposing the hardships of urban laborers to the wealthy middle class. Bennett's sharp critique of industrialization became a model for later social activists.

Bennett faced a significant crisis in the late 1920s when her identity was exposed against her will. Many family members cut ties with her, and she was blacklisted by several major publishers. This period of isolation led her to produce some of her most introspective and poetic works, which gained a cult following in later years.

She continued to be a voice for the voiceless until the end of her life in 1960. Today, she is honored as one of the most courageous figures in literature.

questions.quiz: Why did Bennett choose to write under a masculine pen name?
questions.answer1: So her work would be judged on its own merit
questions.answer2: To keep her family's wealth from becoming public
questions.answer3: To avoid legal trouble for her controversial views
questions.answer4: Because she disliked her real name
questions.commentary: 1단락에 'using a masculine pen name to ensure her work was judged on its own merit'라고 나옵니다. to ensure(~을 보장하기 위해)는 목적을 나타내는 표현입니다. merit(공로, 가치)는 작품 자체의 능력으로 평가받겠다는 의미로, 당시 여성이라는 이유로 차별받는 현실을 반영합니다.

questions.quiz: What was the subject of Bennett's 1920 debut novel?
questions.answer1: The lifestyle of wealthy English families
questions.answer2: The hardships faced by working-class people
questions.answer3: Major historical events in early 20th-century England
questions.answer4: A science fiction story set in the future
questions.commentary: 2단락에 'its raw portrayal of working-class struggles'와 'exposing the hardships of urban laborers'라고 나옵니다. raw portrayal(생생한 묘사)을 hardships(고난)으로 paraphrasing했습니다. 1번 wealthy families의 lifestyle은 지문에서 Bennett이 비판한 대상이지 소설의 주제가 아닙니다.

questions.quiz: What happened to Bennett in the late 1920s?
questions.answer1: She married into a prominent family.
questions.answer3: Her true identity was publicly revealed.
questions.answer4: She was arrested because of her writing.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'her identity was exposed against her will'이라고 나옵니다. against her will(그녀의 의사에 반하여)은 비자발적으로 일어난 일임을 나타내는 중요한 표현입니다. 2번 voluntarily(자발적으로)는 지문 내용과 반대입니다.

questions.quiz: How did Bennett's period of isolation in the late 1920s affect her writing?
questions.answer1: It inspired more introspective and poetic works.
questions.answer2: She permanently lost the ability to write creatively.
questions.answer3: It forced her to write only for financial reasons.
questions.answer4: She shifted to writing exclusively for children.
questions.commentary: 3단락에 'This period of isolation led her to produce some of her most introspective and poetic works'라고 나옵니다. lead A to B(A가 B하도록 이끌다) 구조가 쓰였습니다. 고난이 오히려 더 깊은 작품을 만들어냈다는 역설적 결과를 파악하는 것이 핵심입니다.

questions.quiz: How is Clara Bennett remembered today?
questions.answer1: As a social activist who failed to create change
questions.answer2: As a wealthy writer who lived a life of leisure
questions.answer3: As a controversial figure who damaged the literary world
questions.answer4: As one of the most courageous figures in literature
questions.commentary: 4단락에 'she is honored as one of the most courageous figures in literature'라고 나옵니다. 전기문의 마지막 문장은 인물에 대한 최종 평가를 담고 있어 주제/평가 문제의 정답 근거가 되는 경우가 많습니다. courageous(용감한) = brave = daring 등도 함께 기억하세요.

questions.quiz: The word 'anonymous' in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: hidden from view
questions.commentary: anonymous는 '익명의, 이름이 알려지지 않은'이라는 뜻으로, nameless(이름 없는)가 동의어입니다. anonymity(익명성)라는 명사 형태도 알아두세요. 1번 famous(유명한)는 반의어에 해당합니다.

questions.quiz: The word 'blacklisted' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to:
questions.answer3: banned
questions.commentary: blacklist는 '블랙리스트에 올리다, 배척하다'라는 뜻으로, banned(금지된, 배제된)가 동의어입니다. 1번 celebrated(유명한, 칭송받는)와 4번 promoted(홍보된, 승진한)는 반의어에 가깝습니다. 지문에서 주요 출판사들에게 배척당했다는 맥락으로 파악할 수 있습니다.
